title: caltha palustris subsp. araneosa
intro: Caltha palustris subsp. araneosa, also known as marsh marigold, is a perennial plant species that belongs to the buttercup family. It has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it a potential remedy for various health issues. The medicinal properties of Caltha palustris subsp. araneosa may help alleviate symptoms of fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, providing relief from pain and inflammation. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are often used to promote overall well-being and support the immune system.
